evals.expected_output: Verifies local instance is running, exports all entity models/workflows AND JSON schemas to migration/ directory, invokes setup+login for cloud, imports each workflow, runs /cyoda:test against cloud, instructs user to update app config

assertions.text: Verifies local cyoda-go instance is running before doing anything
Exports all entity workflows to migration/ directory
Exports JSON Schema (field definitions) for each entity via /api/model/export/JSON_SCHEMA/{entity}/{version}
Confirms both schema and workflow files exist for each entity before proceeding
Invokes /cyoda:setup (cloud mode) then /cyoda:auth
Imports each workflow to cloud
Runs /cyoda:test against cloud after import
Instructs user to update app config to point to cloud
Does NOT attempt export without first verifying local instance
Does NOT skip schema export — only exporting workflow is insufficient
Runs Phase 4a GET queries against cloud for each entity before issuing any POST import command
Phase 4a and 4b URLs do NOT contain 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1' — all cloud operations target the cloud endpoint

evals.expected_output: Detects local instance is unreachable, tells user to start cyoda-go first, stops gracefully without attempting export

assertions.text: Detects local instance is unreachable
Output contains the literal string `cyoda` (backtick-quoted command) as the instruction to start the server
Stops gracefully — does not attempt export
Does NOT silently proceed to cloud setup without local data

evals.prompt: /cyoda:migrate — both my Order and Product entities already exist in cloud with old workflows
evals.expected_output: Checks all cloud entities before importing, collects both conflicts in a single summary, asks overwrite or skip once for all conflicts, proceeds based on user choices — does not import until user decides

assertions.text: Queries cloud for existing workflows (Phase 4a) before issuing any POST import command
Shows a diff of the conflicting workflow (state names or transition count differs between local and cloud)
Asks whether to overwrite or skip the conflicting workflow
Presents both Order and Product conflicts in a single combined prompt — no intermediate prompt appears between showing Order's conflict and Product's
Imports only entities the user marked overwrite or new; skips the rest
Does NOT issue any POST import before user has responded to the conflict prompt

evals.expected_output: All entities are new (no cloud workflows exist yet) — pre-check completes with no conflicts and proceeds to import without interrupting the user

assertions.text: Phase 4a GET queries target the cloud endpoint (from post-setup config), not the original local endpoint
Phase 4a and 4b URLs do NOT contain 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1'
Does NOT ask the user overwrite/skip when all entities are new (no cloud workflows exist)
Imports all entities without interruption when no conflicts exist

evals.expected_output: Detects 401 during export, invokes cyoda:auth to refresh, retries the export call once

assertions.text: Invokes cyoda:auth when any export or import call returns 401 or 403
Retries the failed call exactly once after auth refresh
Does NOT retry on 404 or 5xx — only on 401/403
